message.text:
Update #file:tests.yml  workflow to include caching of node_modules and Playwright browser binaries folder utilizing the latest version of 'actions/cache' action. Make sure OS is included in the key generation as well as the hash of the package.lock file.
Additionally make sure that 'Install dependencies' step is only executed if a cache does NOT exist for the execution key.

parts.text:
workflow to include caching of node_modules and Playwright browser binaries folder utilizing the latest version of 'actions/cache' action. Make sure OS is included in the key generation as well as the hash of the package.lock file.
Additionally make sure that 'Install dependencies' step is only executed if a cache does NOT exist for the execution key.

response.value:
The user wants to update the GitHub Actions workflow to:
1. Cache `node_modules` using `actions/cache` (latest version)
2. Cache Playwright browser binaries
3. Include OS in the cache key
4. Include hash of `package-lock.json` in the cache key
5. Skip "Install dependencies" step if cache exists (using cache-hit output)

Let me read the current workflow file first, then make the changes.

The current workflow already shows the content in the attachment. Let me plan the changes:

1. Add a cache step for `node_modules` before "Install dependencies"
2. Add a cache step for Playwright browsers before "Install Playwright Browsers"


3. Make "Install dependencies" conditional on cache miss
4. Make "Install Playwright Browsers" conditional on Playwright cache miss

For the cache keys, I'll use the OS from `runner.os` and the hash of `package-lock.json` using `hashFiles()`. I'm using `actions/cache@v4` which is the latest version. For Playwright, the browser binaries are cached at `~/.cache/ms-playwright`. Now I'll structure the cache steps with appropriate IDs so I can reference the cache-hit output in conditional statements.

Setting up the node_modules cache with the combined key, then making npm ci conditional on a cache miss. For Playwright, I'm doing the same pattern—caching the browser binaries and only running the install command if the cache wasn't hit. I'm thinking both should be conditional since it makes sense to skip installation when we already have cached dependencies. I'll go ahead and add the conditional to both steps since skipping the install on cache hit is essential for the caching to actually work, even though the user only explicitly mentioned "Install dependencies."

edits.text:
Cache node_modules
      id: cache-node-modules
      uses: actions/cache@v4
      with:
        path: node_modules
        key: ${{ runner.os }}-node-modules-${{ hashFiles('**/package-lock.json') }}
    - name: Install dependencies
      if: steps.cache-node-modules.outputs.cache-hit != 'true'
      run: npm ci
    - name: Cache Playwright browsers
      id: cache-playwright-browsers
      uses: actions/cache@v4
      with:
        path: ~/.cache/ms-playwright
        key: ${{ runner.os }}-playwright-${{ hashFiles('**/package-lock.json') }}
    - name: Install Playwright Browsers
      if: steps.cache-playwright-browsers.outputs.cache-hit != 'true'

response.value:
Two cache steps were added:

1. **`Cache node_modules`** — caches
with key `<OS>-node-modules-<hash of

>`. The **Install dependencies** step is skipped on cache hit.
2. **`Cache Playwright browsers`** — caches `~/.cache/ms-playwright` with key `<OS>-playwright-<hash of

>`. The **Install Playwright Browsers** step is also skipped on cache hit (otherwise the cache would be populated but never used).

Both use `actions/cache@v4` and incorporate `runner.os` and `hashFiles('**/package-lock.json')` in their keys, so caches are invalidated whenever dependencies change or the OS differs.

renderedUserMessage.text:
<attachments>
<attachment id="file:tests.yml" filePath="/Users/jgomez/qualitara-take-home/.github/workflows/tests.yml">
name: Playwright Tests
on:
  push:
    branches: [ main, master ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main, master ]
jobs:
  test:
    timeout-minutes: 60
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
      with:
        node-version: lts/*
    - name: Install dependencies
      run: npm ci
    - name: Install Playwright Browsers
      run: npx playwright install --with-deps
    - name: Set up .env.local
      run: |
        cp .env .env.local
        echo "USERNAME=${{ secrets.USERNAME }}" >> .env.local
        echo "PASSWORD=${{ secrets.PASSWORD }}" >> .env.local
    - name: Run Playwright tests
      run: npx playwright test
    - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
      if: ${{ !cancelled() }}
      with:
        name: playwright-report
        path: playwright-report/
        retention-days: 30
</attachment>

</attachments>

edits.text:
Restore dependencies cache
      id: cache-restore
      uses: actions/cache/restore@v4
      with:
        path: |
          node_modules
          ~/.cache/ms-playwright
        key: ${{ runner.os }}-deps-${{ hashFiles('**/package-lock.json') }}
    - name: Install dependencies
      if: steps.cache-restore.outputs.cache-hit != 'true'
      run: npm ci
    - name: Install Playwright Browsers
      if: steps.cache-restore

- name: Save dependencies cache
      id: cache-save
      if: always() && steps.cache-restore.outputs.cache-hit != 'true'
      uses: actions/cache/save@v4
      with:
        path: |
          node_modules
          ~/.cache/ms-playwright
        key: ${{ steps.cache-restore.outputs.cache-primary-key }}

response.value: Let me verify the final result:

Here's what changed, following the [Always save cache](https://github.com/actions/cache/tree/main/save#always-save-cache) pattern:

- **Single `actions/cache/restore@v4`** at the top caches both

and `~/.cache/ms-playwright` under one key (`<OS>-deps-<package-lock hash>`).
- **Both install steps** are skipped when `cache-hit == 'true'`.
- **`actions/cache/save@v4`** is placed at the very end (after the artifact upload) with `if: always() && steps.cache-restore.outputs.cache-hit != 'true'`, so it:
  - Runs even if the workflow fails mid-way (via `always()`)
  - Skips saving if the cache already existed (avoids redundant uploads)
  - Uses `cache-primary-key` from the restore step to guarantee key consistency

name: Playwright Tests
on:
  push:
    branches: [ main, master ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main, master ]
jobs:
  test:
    timeout-minutes: 60
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
      with:
        node-version: lts/*
    - name: Cache node_modules
      id: cache-node-modules
      uses: actions/cache@v4
      with:
        path: node_modules
        key: ${{ runner.os }}-node-modules-${{ hashFiles('**/package-lock.json') }}
    - name: Install dependencies
      if: steps.cache-node-modules.outputs.cache-hit != 'true'
      run: npm ci
    - name: Cache Playwright browsers
      id: cache-playwright-browsers
      uses: actions/cache@v4
      with:
        path: ~/.cache/ms-playwright
        key: ${{ runner.os }}-playwright-${{ hashFiles('**/package-lock.json') }}
    - name: Install Playwright Browsers
      if: steps.cache-playwright-browsers.outputs.cache-hit != 'true'
      run: npx playwright install --with-deps
    - name: Set up .env.local
      run: |
        cp .env .env.local
        echo "USERNAME=${{ secrets.USERNAME }}" >> .env.local
        echo "PASSWORD=${{ secrets.PASSWORD }}" >> .env.local
    - name: Run Playwright tests
      run: npx playwright test
    - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
      if: ${{ !cancelled() }}
      with:
        name: playwright-report
        path: playwright-report/
        retention-days: 30
</attachment>

Here's what changed, following the [Always save cache](https://github.com/actions/cache/tree/main/save#always-save-cache) pattern:

- **Single `actions/cache/restore@v4`** at the top caches both `node_modules` and `~/.cache/ms-playwright` under one key (`<OS>-deps-<package-lock hash>`).
- **Both install steps** are skipped when `cache-hit == 'true'`.
- **`actions/cache/save@v4`** is placed at the very end (after the artifact upload) with `if: always() && steps.cache-restore.outputs.cache-hit != 'true'`, so it:

  - Skips saving if the cache already existed (avoids redundant uploads)
  - Uses `cache-primary-key` from the restore step to guarantee key consistency

## README.md

# Save action
...
## Documentation

Permalink: Documentation 
### Inputs

Permalink: Inputs
• `key` - An explicit key for a cache entry. See [creating a cache key](https://github.com/actions/cache/blob/main/README.md#creating-a-cache-key).
• `path` - A list of files, directories, and wildcard patterns to cache. See [@actions/glob](https://github.com/actions/toolkit/tree/main/packages/glob) for supported patterns.
• `upload-chunk-size` - The chunk size used to split up large files during upload, in bytes

 
### Outputs

Permalink: Outputs
This action has no outputs.

 
...

...
# Save action
...
## Use cases
...
### Only save cache

Permalink: Only save cache
If you are using separate jobs for generating common artifacts and sharing them
across jobs, this action will take care of your cache saving needs.

  steps:
  - uses: actions/checkout@v6

  - name: Install Dependencies
    run: /install.sh

  - name: Build artifacts
    run: /build.sh

  - uses: actions/cache/save@v5
    id: cache
    with:
      path: path/to/dependencies
      key: ${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('**/lockfiles') }} 
    ...

...
# Save action
...
## Use cases
...
### Re-evaluate cache key while saving
...
#### Restore a cache

Permalink: Restore a cache  uses: actions/cache/restore@v5
id: restore-cache
with:
    key: cache-${{ hashFiles('**/lockfiles') }} 
#### Case 1 - Where a user would want to reuse the key as it is

Permalink: Case 1 - Where a user would want to reuse the key as it is  uses: actions/cache/save@v5
with:
    key: ${{ steps.restore-cache.outputs.cache-primary-key }} 
#### Case 2 - Where the user would want to re-evaluate the key

Permalink: Case 2 - Where the user would want to re-evaluate the key  uses: actions/cache/save@v5
with:
    key: npm-cache-${{hashfiles(package-lock.json)}} 
...

...
# Save action
...
## Use cases
...
### Re-evaluate cache key while saving

Permalink: Re-evaluate cache key while saving
With this save action, the key can now be re-evaluated while executing the
action. This helps in cases where lockfiles are generated during the build.


Let's say we have a restore step that computes a key at runtime.

 
#### Restore a cache
...
#### Case 1 - Where a user would want to reuse the key as it is
...
#### Case 2 - Where the user would want to re-evaluate the key
...

...
# Save action
...
## Use cases
...
### Always save cache

Permalink: Always save cache
There are instances where some flaky test cases would fail the entire workflow
and users would get frustrated because the builds would run for hours and the
cache couldn't be saved as the workflow failed in between. For such use-cases,
users now have the ability to use the `actions/cache/save` action to save the cache by using an [always()](https://docs.github.com/actions/writing-workflows/choosing-what-your-workflow-does/expressions#always) condition. This way the cache will always be saved if generated, or a warning
will be generated that nothing is found on the cache path. Users can also use the
`if` condition to only execute the `actions/cache/save` action depending on the output of previous steps. This way they get more
control of when to save the cache.


To avoid saving a cache that already exists, the `cache-hit` output from a restore step should be checked.
...

...
# Save action
...
## Use cases
...
### Always save cache
...
The `cache-primary-key` output from the restore step should also be used to ensure the cache key does
not change during the build if it's calculated based on file contents.


Here's an example where we imagine we're calculating a lot of prime numbers and
want to cache them:

  name: Always Caching Prime Numbers

on: push

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v6

    - name: Restore cached Prime Numbers
      id: cache-prime-numbers-restore
      uses: actions/cache/restore@v5
      with:
        key: ${{ runner.os }}-prime-numbers
        path: |
          path/to/dependencies
          some/other/dependencies

    # Intermediate workflow steps

    - name: Always Save Prime Numbers
      id: cache-prime-numbers-save
      if: always() && steps.cache-prime-numbers-restore.outputs.cache-hit != 'true'
      uses: actions/cache/save@v5
    ...

...
# Save action
...
## Use cases
...
### Always save cache
...
      with:
        key: ${{ steps.cache-prime-numbers-restore.outputs.cache-primary-key }}
        path: |
          path/to/dependencies
          some/other/dependencies    
    ...

...
# Save action
...
## Use cases

Permalink: Use cases 
### Only save cache
...
### Re-evaluate cache key while saving
...
### Always save cache
...

...
# Save action

Permalink: Save action
The save action saves a cache. It works similarly to the `cache` action except that it doesn't first do a restore. This action provides granular
ability to save a cache without having to restore it, or to do a save at any
stage of the workflow job -- not only in post phase.

 
## Documentation
...
## Use cases

<attachments>
<attachment id="file:tests.yml" filePath="/Users/jgomez/qualitara-take-home/.github/workflows/tests.yml">
name: Playwright Tests
on:
  push:
    branches: [ main, master ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main, master ]
jobs:
  test:
    timeout-minutes: 60
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
      with:
        node-version: lts/*
    - name: Restore dependencies cache
      id: cache-restore
      uses: actions/cache/restore@v4
      with:
        path: |
          node_modules
          ~/.cache/ms-playwright
        key: ${{ runner.os }}-deps-${{ hashFiles('**/package-lock.json') }}
    - name: Install dependencies
      if: steps.cache-restore.outputs.cache-hit != 'true'
      run: npm ci
    - name: Install Playwright Browsers
      if: steps.cache-restore.outputs.cache-hit != 'true'
      run: npx playwright install --with-deps
    - name: Set up .env.local
      run: |
        cp .env .env.local
        echo "USERNAME=${{ secrets.USERNAME }}" >> .env.local
        echo "PASSWORD=${{ secrets.PASSWORD }}" >> .env.local
    - name: Run Playwright tests
      run: npx playwright test
    - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
      if: ${{ !cancelled() }}
      with:
        name: playwright-report
        path: playwright-report/
        retention-days: 30
    - name: Save dependencies cache
      id: cache-save
      if: always() && steps.cache-restore.outputs.cache-hit != 'true'
      uses: actions/cache/save@v4
      with:
        path: |
          node_modules
          ~/.cache/ms-playwright
        key: ${{ steps.cache-restore.outputs.cache-primary-key }}
</attachment>

name: Playwright Tests
on:
  push:
    branches: [ main, master ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main, master ]
jobs:
  test:
    timeout-minutes: 60
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
      with:
        node-version: lts/*
    - name: Restore dependencies cache
      id: cache-restore
      uses: actions/cache/restore@v4
      with:
        path: |
          node_modules
          ~/.cache/ms-playwright
        key: ${{ runner.os }}-deps-${{ hashFiles('**/package-lock.json') }}
    - name: Install dependencies
      if: steps.cache-restore.outputs.cache-hit != 'true'
      run: npm ci
    - name: Install Playwright Browsers
      if: steps.cache-restore.outputs.cache-hit != 'true'
      run: npx playwright install --with-deps
    - name: Set up .env.local
      run: |
        cp .env .env.local
        echo "USERNAME=${{ secrets.USERNAME }}" >> .env.local
        echo "PASSWORD=${{ secrets.PASSWORD }}" >> .env.local
    - name: Run Playwright tests
      run: npx playwright test
    - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
      if: ${{ !cancelled() }}
      with:
        name: playwright-report
        path: playwright-report/
        retention-days: 30
    - name: Save dependencies cache
      id: cache-save
      if: always() && steps.cache-restore.outputs.cache-hit != 'true'
      uses: actions/cache/save@v4
      with:
        path: |
          node_modules
          ~/.cache/ms-playwright
        key: ${{ steps.cache-restore.outputs.cache-primary-key }}
</attachment>
